run_summary.summary: Scanned 8 emails across 5 threads. Two updates today: (1) Ian at Popfly re-opened the Flexsteel/Steinhafels campaign application — it closes TOMORROW April 24. Kenny said he'll handle it when they get back from their Steinhafels visit today. Flagging as urgent since missing this deadline could block content submission and payment on the platform. (2) Luna at Yousky replied again today holding firm at $250 for their first collaboration (vs. our $2,000 ask) — the gap is significant and the team needs to decide whether to accept, final-counter, or pass.

suggestions.message: ⚠️ Popfly application closes TOMORROW (April 24). Ian specifically re-opened it for Liz after Kenny thought it was already handled. Kenny said he'd complete it today after Steinhafels. You need to log into Popfly and click 'apply' on the campaign — without that step, you can't upload content drafts or submit an invoice. Don't let this slip overnight!

Luna replied again today with the same answer: $250 max for a first collab. You've asked for $2,000 twice now. The gap isn't closing — what's the call? Accept $250, send one final counter, or let it go?

deals.next_action: Complete Popfly campaign application — DEADLINE TOMORROW April 24
deals.notes: [Apr 23]: Ian reopened Popfly campaign application — closes April 24. Kenny and Liz were at Steinhafels today for filming. Kenny confirmed he'll complete the Popfly application when they get back. Must apply on platform before deadline to enable content draft uploads and invoice submission.

deals.next_action: Decide: accept $250, send final counter, or pass — Luna's cap is firm
deals.notes: [Apr 23]: Luna replied again confirming $250 is their absolute max for a first collaboration. Our standing ask has been $2,000. Rate gap is significant. Team needs to make a call on this one.
